disclaimer: Axes and pro/con bullets are curated analysis for a local-AI comparison, not certified ratings. Verify against primary sources.

entities.name: QVAC SDK (Tether)
entities.tagline: Tether's SDK for on-device inference, agent primitives, and value rails on consumer hardware.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: SDK for building local-first AI apps on consumer devices — models, agents, and rails under one Tether-backed stack.

facts.label: Target
facts.value: Developers building on-device AI products that need privacy, agent coordination, and a path to programmable value.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Tether — one of the largest digital asset issuers globally, funding AI R&D and device-level tooling.

pros.text: Strong corporate backing from Tether with a long-term roadmap spanning models, apps, and settlement rails.
Vertically integrated: SDK + Workbench + vertical apps means a coordinated developer-to-user story.

cons.text: Younger ecosystem vs established OSS runtimes: fewer community integrations and third-party tooling today.

entities.name: Ollama
entities.tagline: One-command local LLM runtime with an OpenAI-compatible API and massive ecosystem.

facts.label: Focus
facts.value: Download and run LLMs locally in one command. Serves an OpenAI-compatible API on localhost.

facts.label: Tech
facts.value: Built on llama.cpp (and now MLX on Apple Silicon in preview). Supports GGUF and NVFP4 formats.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Independent / community-driven with significant developer adoption. No single corporate parent.

pros.text: Lowest-friction way to run local LLMs. Enormous ecosystem — powers coding agents, assistants, and downstream tools.

cons.text: Enterprise packaging (RBAC, audit, SLAs) is DIY. No unified vertical product story — it's a runtime, not a platform.

entities.name: llama.cpp
entities.tagline: The C/C++ foundation for local LLM inference — what most local AI tools build on.

facts.label: Focus
facts.value: Minimal-dependency C/C++ LLM inference engine. llama-cli for chat, llama-server for OpenAI-compatible API.

facts.label: Ecosystem
facts.value: The engine underneath Ollama, LM Studio, LocalAI, and many others. GGUF is the de facto local model format.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Created by Georgi Gerganov. The ggml team joined Hugging Face in early 2026. MIT licensed.

pros.text: Widest hardware support (CUDA, Vulkan, Metal, CPU) and best raw performance. The foundation everything else builds on.

cons.text: Requires building from source or manual setup for full control. Higher barrier to entry than wrapper tools like Ollama.

entities.name: MLX (Apple)
entities.tagline: Apple's open-source array framework for machine learning on Apple Silicon.

facts.label: Focus
facts.value: NumPy-like array framework with Python, C++, and Swift APIs. Optimized for Apple Silicon unified memory and Neural Accelerators.

facts.label: Ecosystem
facts.value: mlx-lm for LLM inference/fine-tuning. Powers Ollama's new MLX backend. Experimental CUDA support for Linux.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Apple Machine Learning Research. MIT licensed. 25k GitHub stars.

pros.text: Best-in-class performance on Apple Silicon. Up to 4x TTFT speedup on M5 with Neural Accelerators.

cons.text: Apple Silicon only for production use. Not a general-purpose cross-platform runtime — use llama.cpp or Ollama for that.

entities.name: LocalAI
entities.tagline: All-in-one open-source AI engine — LLMs, vision, voice, image, and video locally.

facts.label: Focus
facts.value: Go-based engine with 35+ backends (llama.cpp, vLLM, transformers, whisper, diffusers, MLX). OpenAI + Anthropic API compatible.

facts.label: Features
facts.value: Agents, fine-tuning UI, distributed clustering, multi-user auth/quotas (v4.1). No GPU required.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Community-driven, created by Ettore Di Giacinto. MIT licensed. 45k GitHub stars.

pros.text: Most complete local AI stack: LLMs, image gen, TTS, STT, vision, embeddings, agents, and MCP — in a single binary.

cons.text: Breadth comes with complexity. Docker recommended for setup. No major corporate backer — community-maintained.

entities.name: vLLM
entities.tagline: Production-grade LLM serving with PagedAttention — now expanding to consumer devices.

facts.label: Focus
facts.value: High-throughput, memory-efficient LLM inference and serving. PagedAttention, continuous batching, speculative decoding.

facts.label: Consumer use
facts.value: Apple Silicon via vllm-metal plugin (MLX backend). Consumer GPU use via on-demand gateway pattern. Primarily a server-side tool.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Originated at UC Berkeley Sky Lab. Community-driven with broad industry sponsorship. Apache 2.0 licensed. 75k GitHub stars.

pros.text: Highest throughput for GPU serving. Industry standard for production LLM deployments. Broad model and hardware support.

cons.text: Primarily designed for production GPU servers — complex setup, Linux-first. Consumer-device use is secondary and experimental.

entities.name: QVAC Workbench (Tether)
entities.tagline: Tether's end-user AI app — on-device inference, vertical apps, data sovereignty.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: Desktop AI application with Workbench + vertical apps (Health, Translate). Privacy-first: data stays on device.

facts.label: Roadmap
facts.value: Genesis datasets, mobile tooling, and agent coordination features on the horizon.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Tether AI division — spanning models, apps, and infrastructure under one corporate roadmap.

pros.text: Strong data sovereignty story: no cloud dependency as default. Backed by Tether's long-term AI investment.

cons.text: Younger app vs mature alternatives like LM Studio. Smaller model library and community integrations today.

entities.name: LM Studio
entities.tagline: GUI-first local LLMs: discover models, chat, and ship a localhost API.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: Polished desktop app for downloading, evaluating, and serving LLMs locally. Model browser with one-click runs.

facts.label: API
facts.value: OpenAI-compatible API on localhost:1234. Python and TypeScript SDKs reached v1.0.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Closed-source product, free for personal use. Independent company (LM Studio Inc.).

pros.text: Lowest friction for non-terminal users. Excellent model browser and side-by-side evaluation. Mac/Win/Linux.

cons.text: Closed-source. Limited extensibility — no agents, plugins, or MCP by default. Enterprise features (RBAC, audit) absent.

entities.name: Jan
entities.tagline: Open-source ChatGPT replacement — agents, CLI, memory, and full offline support.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: Apache 2.0 desktop app. Built-in foundation models, agents, CLI, and OpenAI-compatible API. 5M+ downloads.

facts.label: Features
facts.value: Agents with tool use, WhatsApp/email connectors, MCP support, native MLX on macOS, context management.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Jan AI (formerly Homebrew Computer Company). Venture-backed indie. Apache 2.0 licensed. 41k GitHub stars.

pros.text: Broadest platform support (Mac/Win/Linux) with agents, CLI, and web UI. Active open-source community.

cons.text: Stability issues in recent releases (v0.7.8 model download bugs). Rapidly evolving — API surface can shift.

entities.name: Osaurus
entities.tagline: Native macOS AI harness — agents, persistent memory, tools, and cryptographic identity.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: Swift-native macOS app. Local models via MLX, cloud providers optional. Agents with 4-layer memory and crypto identity.

facts.label: Features
facts.value: 20+ native plugins, MCP server/client, sandboxed code execution (macOS 26), schedules, watchers, skills.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Osaurus Inc. — indie, built in public. MIT licensed. 4.4k GitHub stars.

pros.text: Most extensible local AI app: agents, memory, plugins, MCP, crypto identity, and sandboxed execution in one package.

cons.text: macOS only (Apple Silicon, macOS 15.5+). Small team, niche audience. No Windows or Linux support.

entities.name: Apple Intelligence
entities.tagline: Private Cloud Compute + on-device models — Apple's bundle, not an open agent platform.

facts.label: Scope
facts.value: On-device models plus selective cloud augmentation. Deep integration with Apple hardware, Siri, and system apps.

facts.label: Extensibility
facts.value: Limited to Apple's extension points. Not a general-purpose agent platform — platform-governed boundaries.

facts.label: Backing
facts.value: Apple Inc. — the largest company by market cap. Massive distribution across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

pros.text: Unmatched distribution and polished on-device UX for consumers within the Apple ecosystem.

cons.text: Closed ecosystem with minimal developer extensibility. Only Apple's own models. No cross-platform story.
